papers.summary: Policing is a stressful profession, and effective leadership is needed to mitigate the effects of organizational stressors that make it one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. Evidence suggests that transformational and transactional leadership can influence the health outcomes and commitment levels of officers, but much of the research and strategies are derived from large jurisdictions and have failed to account for smaller resource constrained contexts. Therefore, the viability of these strategies in smaller jurisdictions such as those in the Caribbean are understudied. Studies have tended to apply quantitative methodologies, which do not give officers a voice to fully capture their contextual experiences that ultimately shape their work related outcomes. To address this gap, more context specific research is needed which captures the voice of officers and investigates how leadership directly impacts organizational commitment and wellness in smaller jurisdictions. papers.summary: Democracy faces critical challenges, including declining trust, misinformation, political polarization, and the strain on election systems. Despite its importance, behavioral science has contributed relatively little to addressing these issues compared to fields like health and sustainability. This thesis seeks to fill that gap by exploring how behavioral science can strengthen democracy through practical, evidence-based interventions. Chapter one reviews the evidence and opportunities. Chapter two demonstrates how operational transparency, using video, print, and email interventions, increases voter trust and turnout, especially among those who have no party preference. Chapter three examines the role of trusted messengers and inoculation techniques, such as prebunking and accuracy nudges, in combating misinformation. papers.title: How Future Cognitive Stakes Affect Promise-Breaking
papers.title_ja: 将来の認知的コスト予測が約束破棄に及ぼす影響
papers.summary: This project tests a model of cooperation in which breaking an explicit cooperative promise (“defection”) carries a cognitive cost, and individuals anticipate and manage that cost when deciding how to act. Participants complete an online experiment (implemented in Gorilla and run on Prolific) that combines memory tasks with a one-shot trust game. The key manipulation is the size of a bonus for a second memory test offered later in the study ($1 vs $20). The central prediction is that a larger downstream bonus will reduce the rate of promise-breaking in the trust game. We will test our primary hypothesis with a preregistered logistic regression predicting Defection from Condition (low vs high bonus), supplemented by a two-proportion test and pre-specified robustness analyses that re-code Defection among promisors only and, descriptively, separate non-promisers from promise-keepers and defectors.

papers.title: The nature and tensions of environmental management control in local authorities
papers.title_ja: 地方自治体における環境管理コントロールの性質と緊張関係
papers.authors: Christophe Maurel, Christophe Favoreu, London Proofreaders
papers.summary: This exploratory research aims to characterize, on the one hand, the concept of environmental management control (EMC) through the practices that underpin it, and, on the other hand, the tensions associated with it and the implementation of these practices in public organizations. Although EMC is attracting growing interest within the public sector, it seems to be facing technical and governance difficulties. A grid for analyzing management tools and the theory of organizational paradoxes is used to address the issue of characterizing EMC practices and the associated tensions in the local public sector. Various municipalities were mobilized to explore and characterize their first experiences of implementing EMC. Our results enable us to characterize both the tools used and the tensions generated between the objectives pursued in terms of resource management, dialogue with the socio-economic environment, and behavioral orientation.

papers.title: The Role of Collective Bricolage in Creating University Governance Structures: The Example of the Galileo Observatory
papers.title_ja: 大学ガバナンス構造の創造におけるコレクティブ・ブリコラージュの役割：ガリレオ天文台の例
papers.summary: This study investigates the concept of “collective bricolage” within the framework of creating a universe science observatory in France. In the face of significant transformations in higher education and research, this article examines how university stakeholders can employ collective bricolage to adapt to institutional mandates for restructuring. Drawing on Lévi-Strauss’s notion of bricolage and the theoretical contributions of Duymedjian and Rüling (2010), this research investigates how this bricolage can be implemented in the absence of conventional or normative space to support the process, and particularly within a context characterized by conflictual relationships. The case study analyzes the collective bricolage process and its components, demonstrating that the dialectical nature of collective bricolage facilitates the creation of robust governance structures through the negotiation and recombination of resources and rationales from the diverse actors involved.

papers.title: AI and the Gothic
papers.title_ja: AI とゴシック
papers.summary: For as long as technology has existed, there have been those who are afraid of its implications and wish to roll back its progress. One way in which this has traditionally been effected is through the use of gothic tropes to render new technologies as frightful or vampiric upon either labour or life. In this chapter, I explore the ways that gothic aspects are applied to the new breed of large language models, broadly dubbed AI. Taking a base history of artificial life and its intersection with gothic literatures, such as Frankenstein, but also more contemporary touchstones, this chapter aims to understand how gothic rhetorics influence societal uptake and acceptance or rejection of new technological inventions. This chapter will naturally also explore the unnaturalness of supposedly "playing God" and creating artificial life, a trope very much rooted in gothic traditions.

papers.summary: In its final report published in 2022, the Colombian Truth Commission documented 121,768 cases of enforced disappearance in the country between 1985 and 2016, with statistical estimation models suggesting over 210,000 cases. The signing of definitive peace agreements with the country’s oldest guerrilla group (FARC-EP) in 2017 may have marked an official end to the armed conflict, yet the issue of enforced disappearances remains highly relevant: not only because disappearances continue to occur today, but also because the country faces memorial debt toward its thousands of missing persons and their families.Enforced disappearance is commonly described as an “invisible” crime, deprived of possible images. Indeed, this form of violence aims both at ensuring the impunity of its perpetrators and at the radical erasure—both physical and identity-based—of its victims, leaving their relatives in a state of uncertainty and terror. In this sense, it constitutes a form of violence devoid of visib...
papers.summary_ja: 本研究は、コロンビアの真実委員会が記録した12万人以上の強制失踪事件を、写真表現を通じて可視化する。見えない暴力と記憶の問題を美的・政治的に問い直す。
papers.novelty_reason: 人権侵害の記録と美術表現を直接結合させ、証言文化と視覚文化の新たな関係を構築している。

papers.title: MISLEADING GAZE: IMAGE AND DESIRE IN THE ROVER
papers.title_ja: 誤導的な視線：『放浪者』における映像と欲望
papers.summary: This paper explores Aphra Behn’s The Rover (1677) as a complex interrogation of gender roles, libertinism, and the commodification of female sexuality in Restoration comedy. Drawing from Thomas Killigrew’s Thomaso, the Wanderer (1664), Behn both inherits and subverts the conventions of the genre, particularly through her treatment of masquerade, gaze, and social identity. Focusing on the figures of Angellica Bianca, Hellena, and Florinda, the study analyses how female characters navigate patriarchal constraints, erotic desire, and the unstable dichotomy between virgin and whore. It also examines Behn’s critique of violent male libertinism, exemplified by the character of Willmore, and her strategic use of theatrical devices such as costuming and misrecognition. The carnival setting, with its temporary suspension of societal norms, serves as a crucial backdrop for exploring the fluidity of gendered performance. Ultimately, the paper argues that The Rover challenges contemporary sexual i...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文はアフラ・ベーン『放浪者』（1677年）を、復古王政期喜劇における性役割、放蕩主義、女性らしさの商品化に関する複雑な問い直しとして分析する。先行テキストとの継承と転覆を追跡する。
papers.novelty_reason: 古典劇作品の既知のジェンダー分析を深化させるが、新たな理論的枠組みを提供していない。
papers.source_name: Cineca Institutional Research Information System (Tor Vergata University)

papers.title: Fashion at the Frontier: Photography, Time and Colonial Modernity on Brazil's Madeira-Mamoré Railroad
papers.title_ja: 辺境のファッション：ブラジル・マデイラ＝マモレ鉄道における写真、時間、植民地的モダニティ
papers.summary: Fashion on the Frontier: Photography, Time and Colonial Modernity on the Madeira-Mamoré Railroad, Brazil (2026) examines the relationship between fashion, photography and the temporal regimes of colonial modernity in the Brazilian Amazon. It explores how land was demarcated with the expansion of the North American frontier in South America, but also how the contours of different ethnic and racialised bodies were delineated and articulated through fashion and via the pseudo-scientific medium of photography. It moves across diverse fields of thought to consider histories and theories of photography and fashion, modernity and coloniality, labour and domesticity, gender and whiteness, whilst deploying innovative and pertinent historical methodologies for thinking about archival hegemonies and silences. By interrogating History as a critical practice in the fashioning of Latin America, her research questions the emphases and erasures that haunt Brazilian fashion histories.
papers.summary_ja: 本研究は、ブラジル・アマゾンにおけるマデイラ＝マモレ鉄道建設期のファッション、写真、植民地的モダニティの時間体制の関係性を検証する。拡張する領域とファッション表現の関連性を探究する。
papers.novelty_reason: ファッション史と植民地史、写真研究の学際的統合により、グローバル・モダニティの新たな地域的現れを照射している。
papers.source_name: University of the Arts London Research Online (University of the Arts London)

papers.title: Russian Historical Anarchism Reconsidered: Crossroads of Modernism, Political Imagination, and Cultural Theory
papers.title_ja: ロシア歴史的アナキズムの再考：モダニズム、政治的想像、文化理論の交差点
papers.summary: This paper offers a comprehensive re-evaluation of Russian historical anarchism, interpreting it not as a marginal ideological strain but as a metaphysical and aesthetic movement, the intensity of which exceeds the constraints of conventional political theory. Tracing its genealogy from Bakunin to the Gordin brothers, and engaging with figures as diverse as Tolstoy, Bely, and Malevich, the study reveals a continuum between ontological rebellion, symbolic sabotage, and the aesthetic production of radical form. Russian anarchism is shown to operate not merely as political refusal but as a cultural and semiotic matrix that undercuts the metaphysics of order and representation. From the radical language of Zaum’ to cosmo-linguistic utopianism, this article delineates anarchism as a modality of epistemic destitution and ontological subversion. Interfacing with Western theories of deconstruction, biopower, and destituent potentiality, the Russian archive reveals anarchism’s deeper vocation: ...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文はロシア歴史的アナキズムを周辺的イデオロギーではなく、メタフィジカルで美学的な運動として再評価する。バクーニンから現代までの系譜を追跡し、従来の政治理論の枠を超えた解釈を提示する。
papers.novelty_reason: 政治思想を美学・形而上学的運動として根本的に再定義することで、思想史研究に新たなパラダイムをもたらす。
papers.source_name: Dépôt institutionnel de l'Université libre de Bruxelles (Université Libre de Bruxelles)

papers.title: Toward an Epistemic Aesthetics of Sound:A Response to Tomas Percival’s Countering Risks: John, David, Edward
papers.title_ja: 音の認識論的美学に向けて：トマス・パーシバルの『リスクに対抗して』への応答
papers.summary: [Toward an Epistemic Aesthetics of Sound:A Response to Tomas Percival’s Countering Risks: John, David, Edward]
papers.summary_ja: 本論文は音響現象の認識論的側面に焦点を当て、音の美学的研究における新たな理論的枠組みを提案する。従来の音響研究の限界を指摘しつつ、認識論的アプローチの可能性を探る。
papers.novelty_reason: 音響研究に認識論的視点を導入するが、哲学的枠組み内での理論的深化であり、完全な新規領域ではない。

papers.title: Curating Hinterland:Reassembling Invisible Work in Art / Science Practices
papers.title_ja: 内陸部のキュレーション：アート/サイエンス実践における見えない労働の再集約

papers.novelty_reason: アート/サイエンス実践における労働の可視化は重要だが、既存の参加型研究や制度批評の延長線上にある。

papers.title: Designing Serious Games on Contemporary Music: A Case Study on Poème Électronique
papers.title_ja: 現代音楽に関する真摯なゲームデザイン：『ポエム・エレクトロニック』のケーススタディ
papers.authors: Vittorio Murtas, Juan-David Rodas, Vincenzo Lombardo
papers.summary: The communication of intangible heritage, especially for the contemporary repertoire, is a hard challenge. Interactive narratives can address this challenge by integrating the stories told by media scholars with physical and virtual engagement devices that display stylistic, cultural, and performative dimensions. In this context, contemporary music is particularly challenging, because of its opening to non-traditional sound timbres, spatial arrangement of the performance, and multimodality of experience.This paper introduces the mixed-reality serious game Edgar à GoGo, designed to foster post-listening engagement and enable players to explore both the sound components and their sequencing within an electroacoustic piece. The piece is a paradigmatic composition of Edgard Varèse, the Poème Électronique (1958), a landmark of electroacoustic music originally conceived for the Philips Pavilion at the Brussels World's Fair. This piece was a component of the multimedia show conceived by Le Co...
papers.summary_ja: 本研究は、無形文化遺産、特に現代レパートリーの伝達という課題に対して、インタラクティブなナラティブを活用したシリアスゲーム開発を探究する。スタイル、文化、パフォーマンス要素の統合を実現する。
papers.novelty_reason: 音楽教育にゲーム理論を応用するが、既存のデジタル教育学の枠組み内での実装である。
papers.source_name: Zenodo (CERN European Organization for Nuclear Research)

papers.title: Piano music of the Black Renaissance
papers.title_ja: 黒人ルネッサンスのピアノ音楽
papers.summary: This ground breaking anthology introduces a canon of piano works from the Black Renaissance school of the early twentieth century. The music of Harry T. Burleigh, Samuel Coleridge-Taylor, R. Nathaniel Dett, Nora Holt, Florence B. Price, Zenobia Powell Perry, Margaret Bonds, and Betty Jackson King affirms a rich, Afrodiasporic concert tradition, in which African, Caribbean, and African American folk songs and dances are absorbed into a distinct classical voice. This volume offers clearly presented performing editions, with fingering and pedalling, of a representative selection of piano music from this period, ranging from intermediate to advanced levels. Notes by Dr Samantha Ege provide historical background and interpretative insights for this previously overlooked area of piano literature.
papers.summary_ja: 本アンソロジーは20世紀初頭の黒人ルネッサンス派による画期的なピアノ作品群を紹介し、従来のクラシック音楽史から除外されていた重要な作曲家たちの作品を正典化する。
papers.novelty_reason: 西洋音楽史の正典を根本的に再構成し、黒人作曲家の作品を中心に据えることで、音楽史記述そのものをパラダイムシフトさせている。

papers.title: Jacques Lecoq’s Embodied and Ecological Actor Training
papers.title_ja: ジャック・ルコック『身体化された生態的俳優訓練法』
papers.authors: Evans, Mark; id_orcid 0000-0003-0564-0771, Pearce, Michael, Kenney, Denise
papers.summary: This chapter argues that Jacques Lecoq’s pedagogy, which nurtures creative expression through embodied identification with the more-than-human world and operates through non-hierarchical group creation, can be understood as a form of ecological enquiry, one that functions less as a fixed technique and more as an epistemology: a living methodology.  Jacques Lecoq founded his school in Paris in 1956. Over the last seventy years, it has trained over 5,000 graduates from more than 80 countries, many of whom have gone on to teach and adapt his approach across the globe. Lecoq’s pedagogy offers students a distinctive journey of creative self-discovery. Unlike mainstream theatre programmes that centre on human psychological and social relations, Lecoq’s approach is predicated on the maxim tout bouge (‘everything moves’), a philosophy that invites students to experience their human selves as an integral part of a field of lively and vibrant organic and inorganic materials, rhythms, colours, sp...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文はジャック・ルコックの教育学を、人間以上の世界との身体的同一化を育み、非階層的な集団創作を通じて機能する生態学的問い直しとして再解釈する。固定的技法ではなく、流動的探究実践として位置づける。
papers.novelty_reason: 劇優養成を生態的関係性の問い直しとして根本的に再定義し、人間中心的演劇教育の枠を超えた新しい視点を提供する。

papers.title: ‘It Smelt Real’:Jorvik Viking Centre and the Experience of Museum Smellscapes
papers.title_ja: 『本当の臭いがした』：ヨーリック・バイキング・センターと博物館の臭覚空間の経験
papers.summary: Despite the emergence of sensory history and the increasing use of smell at museums and heritage sites, little attention has been paid to how smell experiences and public perceptions of the past mutually shape each other. Using newspapers, user-generated reviews, participant observation, and surveys, this article provides an analysis of smell’s use and reception at Jorvik Viking Centre, one of the first UK museums to use artificial ambient scents, from its opening in 1984 to the present. Both distributions of the sensible and longer histories of smell media have shaped the way academics, museums professionals, and visitors have responded to Jorvik’s smells. In contrast to existing work that has dismissed the use of smell in museums and heritage spaces as a curatorial trick that contrasts a foul-smelling past with contemporary odorlessness, this article argues that Jorvik’s scents offer an example of a ‘key smell’ that allows for the exploration of multiple overlapping experiences of th...
papers.summary_ja: 本研究は、感覚史研究の台頭にもかかわらず、臭覚経験と過去に関する公衆認識の相互形成についてほとんど注目されていない問題を扱う。新聞、ユーザーレビュー、参与観察調査から臭いと記憶の関係を解明する。
papers.novelty_reason: 博物館研究において臭覚を主要な感覚モダリティとして位置づけることで、多感覚的文化遺産理解の新しい領域を開拓している。
papers.source_name: White Rose Research Online (University of Leeds, The University of Sheffield, University of York)

papers.title: James Ensor’s Veiled Modernity – Re-imagining a Liminal Urban Art
papers.title_ja: ジェームス・アンソール『ヴェールに包まれたモダニティ』―周縁的都市芸術の再想像
papers.summary: James Ensor’s omnivorous interest in the art of the past to rework modern art, as acknowledged, is a pivotal inspiration for his distinctive, uncanny vision of cultural modernity. His deep cross-temporal attraction to late medieval, Gothic art, especially from c. 1900s however, is less well treated, and is the core concern of this article. Discussion opens new insights into the allure of pre-modern art for Ensor’s creative practice, in the context of his exposure to the 1902 Bruges ‘Primitifs Flamands’ exhibition and its array of Flemish so-called ‘primitive’ and early German art, as well as in relation to Ensor’s interactions with a circle of artists and more globalizing interests beyond Belgium. Taking as its focus, Ensor’s evolving response to the late 19th-century urban-scape, the paper explores his potent re-workings of the medieval Dance of Death, cathedral, Christ as the Man of Sorrows and Marian iconographies, to transform the modern city and artistic experience of it into a si...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文はアンソールの過去の芸術を現代美術に組み替える雑食的関心に着目し、特に1900年代以降の後期中世・ゴシック美術への時間を超えた引力が、彼の文化的モダニティの不気味なヴィジョンを形作ったことを論証する。
papers.novelty_reason: アンソールの美学における時間性と歴史参照に新たな光を当てるが、既存の美術史的分析の枠内での深化である。

papers.title: Writing as Spiritual Practice: Beyond Dominant Narratives.
papers.title_ja: 精神的実践としての執筆：支配的言説を超えて
papers.summary: This PhD in Creative and Critical Writing – Writing as Spiritual Practice: Beyond Dominant Narratives – explores addiction as a spiritual and developmental condition shaped by extractive corporate capitalism and considers how memoir can both reflect and resist dominant recovery narratives. Drawing on my lived experience of addiction and recovery, as well as my work as a peer support outreach worker, the project examines writing as a spiritual and political practice capable of unsettling ideological narratives that commodify suffering and reinforce neoliberal ideas of the self. The project comprises two interdependent components: a 40,000-word dissertation and a memoir, Practicing Dying. These are not separate entities, but two ways of making the same argument. Where Practicing Dying emerges from embodied experience, the critical component situates that experience within broader political, spiritual, and theoretical frames. Rather than seeking to prove the memoir’s insights in academic ...
papers.summary_ja: 本博士論文は、抽出的企業資本主義に形成された精神的・発達的条件としての中毒を探究し、回顧録がいかに支配的な回復言説を反映し抵抗するかを考察する。創造的執筆を社会変革の手段として位置づける。
papers.novelty_reason: 執筆を精神的実践かつ政治的抵抗の形態として再定義し、従来の創作教育の枠組みに新たな次元を付与している。

papers.title: Theatre of the Tech-Oppressed: Exploring algorithmic injustice through participatory performances
papers.title_ja: テック抑圧の劇場：参加型パフォーマンスを通じたアルゴリズム的不正義の探究
papers.authors: Babai, Najme, Duvall, Eva, Iivari, Netta, Kinnula, Marianne, Iversen, Ole Sejer; id_orcid 0000-0002-8055-6716
papers.summary: As artificial intelligence (AI) becomes embedded in systems that shape everyday decisions, it is critical to engage young people in making sense of these technologies and their sociotechnical implications. This paper explores how participatory, theater-based methods, particularly Theater of the Oppressed, can foster critical dialogues among youth about algorithmic decision-making, bias, and injustice. Drawing on a workshop with 15–17-year-olds, we analyze forum theater performances that surfaced questions about power, merit, data, and justice in AI systems for decision-making. Through reflective thematic analysis of the performances and spectactor interventions, we identify how participants impersonated algorithmic agents, questioned meritocratic ideals, exposed the role of data in enabling bias, and experimented with hierarchical and imaginative strategies for intervention. Our findings demonstrate the potential of embodied, performative approaches to support computational empowerment...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文は、AIが日常的意思決定を形作るシステムに組み込まれる中、若い世代がこれらの技術と社会技術的含意を理解するための参加型演劇的方法の有効性を探究する。抑圧の演劇を活用したAIリテラシー教育を提案する。
papers.novelty_reason: 演劇的アクティビズムとAI倫理教育の創新的統合により、デジタル時代の市民的参加のための新しい方法論を開拓している。

papers.title: Re-imagining Fashion Histories: Enacting Decolonial Commitments in Institutional Spaces
papers.title_ja: ファッション歴史の再想像：制度的空間における脱植民地化的約束の実践
papers.summary: This chapter explores how decolonial commitments are enacted and contested in institutional spaces, and the possibilities and tensions this can generate. It examines as a case study the ‘Re-imagining Fashion Histories: Tracing Parallel Cosmologies’ module co-taught at Central Saint Martins, London on the MA Fashion Communication course in 2024/25. This module foregrounded the otherwise in fashion historiography to animate and interrogate ‘imagining’ as a practice that constitutes and reinforces coloniality but simultaneously forges decolonial potentialities. ‘Imagining’ acts to define the suppositions, assumptions and representations that have shaped the canon of fashion history, but simultaneously to re-imagine alternative fashion futures beyond an Anglo-Eurocentric and anthropocentric bias. We employ time as a theoretical entry point into discussions concerning power and coloniality. Our contention is that to re-imagine is not to go backwards, but to radically undo, unravel and delin...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文は、制度的空間において脱植民地化的約束がいかに実践され、異議を唱えられるかを探究し、この実践が生み出す可能性と緊張性を検討する。ロンドン・セントラル・セント・マーティンズでの共同教育実践をケーススタディとする。
papers.novelty_reason: ファッション教育機関における脱植民地化実践の具体的検証を通じ、美術高等教育の政治化と多元的知の統合における新たな道筋を示唆している。
papers.source_name: University of the Arts London Research Online (University of the Arts London)

papers.title: The seminal proteome of a monandrous fly, Drosophila subobscura
papers.title_ja: 一妻一夫制のハエ（Drosophila subobscura）の精液プロテオーム
papers.summary: When females mate with multiple males, ejaculates from different partners compete within the female reproductive tract. Males gain a competitive advantage by transferring seminal fluid proteins (Sfps) that manipulate female post-mating responses, such as reducing female propensity to remate. But in monandrous species, where females typically mate only once, post-mating sexual selection is absent, raising key questions about the complexity and identity of Sfps in monandrous seminal proteomes. To address this, we characterised the seminal proteome of the monandrous fly Drosophila subobscura. Using long-read RNA-seq, we created improved gene models for the D. subobscura accessory gland and ejaculatory duct, then, using a label-free quantitative proteomics approach, we identified 172 Sfps. This number is comparable to the 153 Sfps previously reported in polyandrous D. melanogaster using a similar approach. The D. subobscura seminal proteome contains all the D. melanogaster sex peptide netw...
papers.summary_ja: 複数オスとの交配時に精液内のタンパク質が雌の交配後の反応を操作することで、オス同士の競争優位性が生じる。本研究では、一妻一夫制のハエにおける精液タンパク質の役割を調査している。
papers.novelty_reason: 精液タンパク質の機能研究は既知だが、一妻一夫制種における比較は新しい視点を提供する。

papers.title: A Human Pan-Disease Whole Blood Transcriptomics Atlas Reveals Systemic Signatures Across Diseases
papers.title_ja: 人間の全血液トランスクリプトミクス・パンディジーズアトラス：疾患横断的なシステムシグネチャーの解明
papers.summary: This dataset accompanies the manuscript titled “A Human Pan-Disease Whole Blood Transcriptomics Atlas Reveals Systemic Signatures Across Diseases” Whole-blood transcriptomics (WBT) provides critical insights into systemic health and disease. In this study, we established a large-scale WBT Atlas comprising 4,444 samples across 98 distinct health conditions. Through integrative analyses, we identified disease-specific gene expression signatures and developed a multi-omics classification framework capable of distinguishing among these conditions based on their unique transcriptomic profiles. The dataset includes RNA-seq data from 4,444 samples used for atlas construction, along with 10 cohorts utilized for machine learning–based external validation.
papers.summary_ja: 全血液トランスクリプトミクスは全身の健康と疾患に関する重要な知見を提供する。本研究は大規模なWBTアトラスを構築し、複数疾患における共通のシステムシグネチャーを同定した。
papers.novelty_reason: 複数疾患を横断的に統合分析するアトラス構築により、疾患間の共通生物学的メカニズム解明に向けた新たな枠組みを示唆している。
papers.source_name: Zenodo (CERN European Organization for Nuclear Research)

papers.title: The Spatiotemporal evolutionary dynamics of crickets and their influencing factors (Orthoptera; Ensifera)
papers.title_ja: コオロギの時空間的進化動態とその影響因子の解明（直翅目；キリギリス亜目）
papers.authors: Zheng, Yanna, Nie, Yimeng, Liu, Xuanzeng, Ma, Libin, Huang, Yuan
papers.summary: The mechanisms underlying the formation of species diversity have remained a subject of intense debate. In this study, we used Gryllidea (crickets) as a representative lineage and employed a phylogenetic comparative framework based on a robust phylogeny reconstructed from ultraconserved elements, mitochondrial genomes, nuclear ribosomal genes, DNA barcodes, and morphological traits. Our spatiotemporal evolutionary analyses indicate Gryllidea originated in the Early Jurassic, diverging into Grylloidea and Gryllotalpoidea, with marked increases in diversification rates at the ancestral node of Grylloidea and within Trigonidiinae and Goniogryllus of Gryllinae. Most species within these rapidly diversifying clades are distributed in the Oriental and Sino-Japanese regions. Integrating biotic (genomic and morphological) and abiotic (climatic and geographic) factors, we reveal that cricket diversification results from synergistic interactions of multiple factors, with biotic factors exerting ...
papers.summary_ja: 種多様性の形成メカニズムは重要な研究課題である。本研究ではコオロギをモデルにして、超保存配列に基づいた堅牢な系統樹を用いた系統比較解析を行った。
papers.novelty_reason: 超保存配列を用いた系統解析は新しい手法だが、既存の系統地理学的枠組み内での研究である。

papers.title: Differentiated genomic footprints and connections inferred from 440 Hmong-Mien genomes suggest their isolation and long-distance migration
papers.title_ja: 440個のモン・クメール族ゲノムから推測される差別化したゲノム足跡と移動パターン：孤立と長距離移動の証拠
papers.summary: Background: The underrepresentation of Hmong-Mien (HM) people in Asian genomic studies has hindered our comprehensive understanding of the full landscape of their evolutionary history and complex trait architecture. South China is a multi-ethnic region and indigenously settled by ethnolinguistically diverse HM, Austroasiatic (AA), Tai-Kadai (TK), Austronesian (AN) and Sino-Tibetan (ST) people, which is regarded as East Asia's initial cradle of biodiversity. However, previous fragmented genetic studies have only presented a fraction of the landscape of genetic diversity in this region, especially the lack of haplotype-based genomic resources. The deep characterization of demographic history and natural-selection-relevant genetic architecture of HM people was necessary. Results: We reported one HM-specific genomic resource and comprehensively explored the fine-scale genetic structure and adaptative features inferred from the genome-wide SNP data of 440 HM individuals from 33 ethnolinguis...
papers.summary_ja: モン・クメール族はアジアゲノム研究で過少評価されている。本研究は440個のゲノムを分析し、彼らの進化史と複雑形質アーキテクチャを包括的に理解した。
papers.novelty_reason: 未開拓の民族集団の大規模ゲノム解析により、アジア人の進化史における新たな人口遺伝学的シグネチャーを発見している。

papers.title: Dynamic Mitochondrial Protein Succinylation Induced by Nutritional Intervention with Omega-3 Polyunsaturated Fatty Acids Elicits Growth Inhibition and Lethality in Prostate Cancer Cells
papers.title_ja: オメガ3多価不飽和脂肪酸による栄養介入が誘導する動的ミトコンドリアタンパク質スクシニル化がもたらす前立腺癌細胞の成長阻害と細胞死
papers.summary: Prostate cancer (PCa) continues to present a significant therapeutic challenge due to its metabolic plasticity and resistance to conventional treatments. Although omega-3 fatty acids, especially high-purity eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA), have demonstrated anticancer properties, the underlying molecular mechanisms remain inadequately understood. In this study, we provide evidence that EPA induces potent growth inhibition in PCa cells by modulating a mitochondrial succinylation network, thereby revealing three interconnected nodes of metabolic vulnerability. Through succinylomic profiling and functional validation, we identified SDHA, GOT2, and DAP3 as critical EPA-induced succinylation targets. Mechanistically, SDHA succinylation induces succinate accumulation and promotes widespread succinylation across mitochondrial proteins, thereby disrupting the homeostasis of mitochondria. This mitochondrial dysfunction converges on two lethal pathways in PCa cells: first, GOT2 hyper-succinylation i...
papers.summary_ja: 前立腺癌は代謝可塑性と治療耐性が課題である。本研究はエイコサペンタエン酸によるスクシニル化修飾がミトコンドリアの機能変化を通じた抗癌効果の分子基盤を解明した。
papers.novelty_reason: オメガ3脂肪酸の抗癌作用は既知だが、タンパク質スクシニル化という特定の翻訳後修飾メカニズムの同定は新しい視点である。

papers.title: DUB regulators of YAP/TAZ activity in mesothelioma and head and neck cancer
papers.title_ja: 悪性中皮腫および頭頸部癌におけるYAP/TAZ活性の脱ユビキチン化酵素レギュレーター
papers.summary: The Hippo pathway is evolutionarily conserved to regulate multiple biological processes including development, organ size, apoptosis, and tissue regeneration, thereby maintaining tissue homeostasis. This tumour-suppressive signalling cascade acts inactivates the downstream effectors and oncogenes YAP/TAZ, preventing their binding to the TEAD transcription factors. Disruption of the Hippo pathway and aberrant YAP/TAZ activity are observed in many cancers such as pleural mesothelioma (PM) and head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC), where there are known genetic drivers. Deubiquitinating enzymes (DUBs) have been identified as direct or indirect regulators of the pathway. However, questions remain unresolved, notably around the specificity of their interaction with the Hippo components which may depend on the cancer type and/or genetic background. To uncover novel roles for DUBs in malignancies with YAP/TAZ aberrant activation, the regulation of these oncogenes in PM and HNSCC was i...
papers.summary_ja: ヒッポー経路は発生、器官サイズ、アポトーシス、組織再生を制御する進化的に保存された経路である。本研究はYAP/TAZの脱ユビキチン化による活性制御が腫瘍形成に及ぼす影響を調査した。
papers.novelty_reason: ヒッポー経路の癌生物学への応用は既知だが、脱ユビキチン化という特定の修飾機構による制御は新しい標的提唱である。

papers.title: Analysis of cell-free DNA for cancer diagnostics using liquid biopsies
papers.title_ja: リキッドバイオプシーを用いた癌診断のための無細胞DNA解析
papers.summary: Chromatin organisation influences gene regulation and genome stability, yet its dysregulation in cancer remains incompletely understood. This has become particularly important for patient diagnostics using cell-free DNA (cfDNA) from body fluids, based on computational analyses of nucleosome occupancy landscapes reconstructed from cfDNA. This thesis establishes the first comprehensive atlas of nucleosome positioning signatures across repetitive elements in tumour tissues and cfDNA. Using original approaches to cfDNA analysis, I demonstrate that genomic repetitive elements, including satellite repeats, LINEs, SINEs, and LTRs, undergo reproducible family-, subfamily-, and tissue-specific nucleosome repositioning in cancer. Integration with DNA methylation profiling reveals coordinated changes in methylation and nucleosome positioning at specific genomic repeats, underscoring the epigenetic interplay that drives chromatin instability. Quantifying cfDNA profiles at DNA sequence repeats, inc...
papers.summary_ja: クロマチン構成は遺伝子制御とゲノム安定性に影響するが、癌での不調和は不完全に理解されている。本研究はヌクレオソーム占有率の計算解析に基づく無細胞DNA診断の臨床応用を検討した。
papers.novelty_reason: リキッドバイオプシーは既知だが、クロマチン構造の異常を癌診断マーカーとして体系的に統合する新たなアプローチを示している。

papers.title: Agta hunter-gatherer oral microbiomes are shaped by contact network structure
papers.title_ja: アグタ狩猟採集民の口腔マイクロバイオームは接触ネットワーク構造により形成される
papers.authors: Federico Musciotto, Begoña Dobón, Michael Greenacre, Álex Mira, Nikhil Chaudhary, Guz Deniz Salali, Pascale Gerbault, Rodoplh Schlaepfer, Leonora H. Astete, Marilyn Ngales
papers.summary: Here we investigate the effects of extensive sociality and mobility on the oral microbiome of 138 Agta hunter-gatherers from the Philippines. Our comparisons of microbiome composition showed that the Agta are more similar to Central African Bayaka hunter-gatherers than to neighbouring farmers. We also defined the Agta social microbiome as a set of 137 oral bacteria (only 7% of 1980 amplicon sequence variants) significantly influenced by social contact (quantified through wireless sensors of short-range interactions). We show that large interaction networks including strong links between close kin, spouses, and even unrelated friends, can significantly predict bacterial transmission networks across Agta camps. Finally, we show that more central individuals to social networks are also bacterial supersharers. We conclude that hunter-gatherer social microbiomes are predominantly pathogenic and were shaped by evolutionary tradeoffs between extensive sociality and disease spread.
papers.summary_ja: フィリピンのアグタ狩猟採集民138名の口腔マイクロバイオームを調査した。アグタの微生物組成は近隣の農耕民よりも中央アフリカのバヤカ狩猟採集民と類似していることが明らかになった。
papers.novelty_reason: 人間の社会構造と微生物群集の関係性を、接触ネットワークという定量的指標で解析する新たな学際的アプローチを提示している。

papers.title: Tumor-derived SERPINB2 and SERPINE1 orchestrate spatially dominant immunosuppressive niches in pancreatic cancer
papers.title_ja: 腫瘍由来のSERPINB2およびSERPINE1が統合する膵臓癌の空間的に優位な免疫抑制ニッチ
papers.summary: PDAC forms a dense, T-cell-excluded tumor microenvironment, but the molecular drivers of this niche remain unclear. Using Perturb-map, a spatial functional genomics platform, we identify the fibrinolysis regulators Serpinb2 (PAI-2) and Serpine1 (PAI-1) as key mediators of immune evasion. Tumor-derived SERPINs stabilize a fibrin-rich ECM that anchors macrophages, promotes immunosuppressive polarization, and excludes cytotoxic T cells. Their deletion suppresses tumor growth and sensitizes PDAC to anti-PD-1 therapy.
papers.summary_ja: 膵臓癌は密集した腫瘍微小環境を形成するが、その分子的駆動因子は不明である。本研究は空間機能ゲノミクスプラットフォーム(Perturb-map)により、線溶系制御因子SerpinB2およびSerpinE1が免疫逃避の主要仲介因子であることを同定した。
papers.novelty_reason: 空間的機能ゲノミクスという新たな解析手法により、従来の腫瘍免疫学では検出困難な局所的な免疫抑制メカニズムを同定している。

papers.title: Tumor-promoting roles of the chromatin-associated DEK oncogene in metastatic melanoma
papers.title_ja: 転移性メラノーマにおけるクロマチン関連DEKオンコジーンの腫瘍促進機能
papers.summary: As a multifunctional, conserved yet unique non-histone chromatin-architectural factor, the DEK oncoprotein is known to be involved in multiple nuclear processes, including chromatin remodeling, transcriptional regulation, and DNA damage response. Although aberrant DEK expression has been identified in various cancers, its precise mechanistic contribution to tumorigenesis remains incompletely understood. This includes its genome-wide chromatin binding patterns in cancer cells and how this affects chromatin structure and cellular functions. Therefore, the aim of this thesis is to investigate the chromatin binding landscape and functional role of DEK in chromatin organization and transcriptional regulation with a particular focus on phosphorylation-dependent modulation by the casein kinase 2 (CK2) in cancer cells, particularly melanoma cell lines. Using chromatin immunoprecipitation followed by next-generation sequencing (ChIP-seq), we first mapped the genome-wide DEK occupancy in HeLa S3...

papers.novelty_reason: DEKの癌生物学的役割は既知だが、メラノーマ転移という特定の病態への機構解明は既存の腫瘍生物学的枠組み内である。
